reasoning: Wdr48 (also called UAF1) functions primarily as a regulatory co‑factor for several deubiquitinating enzymes (USP1, USP12, USP46) and is involved in DNA‑damage response and cell‑cycle control. Its known actions are post‑translational, influencing protein stability rather than transcriptional programs. Mylip (IDOL) is an E3 ubiquitin ligase whose expression is transcriptionally controlled by liver X receptors (LXRs) in response to cellular cholesterol levels. There is no reported pathway linking Wdr48 activity to LXR signaling or to the transcription of Mylip. Consequently, reducing Wdr48 levels by CRISPRi is unlikely to cause a consistent up‑ or down‑regulation of Mylip mRNA; any effect would be indirect and minimal.
